summary: Issue #463 adds reversible DDL-only migration 018 with four nullable analytics interval backfill columns, matching SQLAlchemy model fields, unit contract tests, and integration migration coverage. Intent-review spec_fidelity=pass. No security, reliability, or performance blockers for schema-only additive nullable columns; RLS and unique index unchanged.

interfaceChanges.description: Adds nullable live_hours, live_sessions, active_products, new_products fields for Phase 2.9 backfill contract.
DDL-only Alembic revision adding four nullable columns on analytics_performance_intervals.

mappings.criterion: Alembic revision adds exactly the four nullable columns above (or documented equivalent names) to analytics_performance_intervals.
upgrade / downgrade are reversible; migration is DDL-only (no row data migration).
AnalyticsPerformanceInterval model exposes nullable backfill columns on analytics_performance_intervals.
Migration integration test proves backfill columns exist after upgrade at Alembic head.
Migration 018 adds nullable backfill columns documented as shared Phase 2.6/2.9/3 contract in ADR/PRD.

recommendations: Issue #464 must rebase Alembic down_revision to 018_analytics_interval_backfill_cols before merge (documented in implementation artifact).

reviewerSignoff.statement: Intent-review passed; Guardrails found no CRITICAL reliability, security, observability, or performance gaps for DDL-only nullable column addition.

ownerSignoff.statement: All five acceptance criteria mapped to tests or docs; schema-only scope respected.
